decisions.title: W0 baseline timings and environment
decisions.description: Environment: macOS + Go workspace (`go.work`), command shell `zsh`, date 2026-02-13. Baseline timings: `make build` real 3.31s; `make test-workspace` real 17.78s (warm cache); `make test-workspace GO_TEST_FLAGS="-race -covermode=atomic -count=1"` real 31.27s (no cache); `make invariants-check` real 0.06s; `make docs-check` failing in 0.30s due header compliance drift; `make proto-lint` fails in 0.01s (`buf` missing); `make proto-gen` fails in 0.01s (missing `./bin/protoc-gen-go`). Commands were executed from repo root.

decisions.title: Hotspot baseline from uncached workspace tests
decisions.description: Top package durations from uncached run (`-count=1`): `internal/adapters/jetstream` 10.226s (largest), `internal/actors/marketdata/ws` 2.711s, `internal/shared/replay` 2.480s, `internal/actors/marketdata/runtime` 2.419s, `internal/shared/naming` 2.370s. This establishes primary optimization focus on partitioning heavy integration tests and preserving strong full-workspace gate.

decisions.title: Current gate authority and enforcement points
decisions.description: Gate authority confirmed from docs and scripts: `make docs-check`, `make invariants-check`, `make test-workspace`, `make test-workspace-race`, `make proto-lint`, `make proto-breaking`, `make soak-check` (Execution Sequence + System Invariants + TRUTH-MAP). Commit message validation is enforced by `.pre-commit-config.yaml` hook `commit-msg-conventional` using `scripts/validate-commit-msg.sh`; currently not exposed as dedicated Make target.

decisions.title: Review hypotheses and acceleration interventions
decisions.description:
H1: Full `test-workspace` always runs heavy integration (`internal/adapters/jetstream` 10.226s) in local loop. Intervention: add `make test-unit` (core/actors/shared excluding integration-marked tests) + keep `make test-workspace` strong gate. Impact: High. Risk: accidental undercoverage if developers stop running full gate. Verification gate: `make test-unit` + `make test-workspace` in CI/pre-push.
H2: No deterministic trigger for replay golden; expensive tests may run too often or too little. Intervention: add explicit `make test-replay-golden` and path-based trigger rule for sequencer/envelope/storage touched files. Impact: Medium. Risk: drift if path filter misses transitive replay-affecting files. Verification gate: `make test-replay-golden`.
H3: Module iteration depends on `go work edit -json | jq`; tooling dependency (`jq`) can break determinism on clean environments. Intervention: harden `scripts/list-modules.sh` fallback parser or preflight check target. Impact: Medium. Risk: parser mismatch with future go.work format. Verification gate: `make modules` + `make test-workspace`.
H4: `docs-check` currently fails hard due broad historical backlog, reducing usefulness as fast deterministic gate. Intervention: split `docs-check` into `docs-check-fast` (changed-files scoped) and `docs-check-full` (strict). Impact: High for local speed. Risk: policy drift if full gate is skipped in CI. Verification gate: `make docs-check-fast` locally and `make docs-check-full` in strong gate.
H5: Proto toolchain missing by default (`buf`/`protoc-gen-go`), causing non-deterministic failures. Intervention: add `make proto-tools` bootstrap and `proto-check` dirty-tree validator; keep `proto-gen` explicit. Impact: High determinism. Risk: version pin drift. Verification gate: `make proto-check`.
H6: Commit message validation only via pre-commit hook; easy to bypass in local scripts/CI. Intervention: add explicit Make target `make commit-msg-check MSG_FILE=...` and include in local pre-push aggregate. Impact: Medium. Risk: false confidence if not wired into workflow target. Verification gate: `make commit-msg-check`.
H7: Race mode always coupled to broad workspace target in CI path; costly for local loop. Intervention: introduce `make test-race` targeted set (actors/adapters high-risk) while preserving `make test-workspace-race` strong gate. Impact: Medium. Risk: hidden races outside target set. Verification gate: `make test-race` + periodic `make test-workspace-race`.
H8: No single fast aggregate command for developers. Intervention: add `make quick` = `fmt-check + vet subset + test-unit + invariants-check` with no proto/docker side effects. Impact: High. Risk: developers treating quick as release gate. Verification gate: `make quick` and release gate remains `make test-workspace`.
H9: Docker compose build integrated in runtime commands (`up`/`docker-up`) can hide slow path costs and network variance. Intervention: isolate optional `make docker-build-bench` and keep out of quick loop. Impact: Low/Medium. Risk: stale container images if ignored. Verification gate: `make docker-build-bench` (manual evidence checkpoint).

decisions.title: Atomic commit chain proposal for build acceleration
decisions.description:
C1 `build(make): add quick local loop target`.
Objective: create fast local gate (`quick`) running fmt/vet subset + test-unit + invariants-check.
Files (<=3): `Makefile`, `scripts/for-each-module.sh` (if needed for subset selector), `README.md` (target note).
Required gates: `make quick`, `make test-workspace`.
Rollback: `git revert <C1-hash>`.
MCP checkpoint: record quick runtime and pass/fail parity versus `test-workspace`.

C2 `build(test): partition test targets unit/integration/race/soak`.
Objective: separate fast deterministic unit path from heavy integration/adapters; keep full gates.
Files: `Makefile`, `scripts/for-each-module.sh`, `scripts/soak-test.sh` (only if wiring).
Required gates: `make test-unit`, `make test-integration`, `make test-race`, `make test-workspace`.
Rollback: `git revert <C2-hash>`.
MCP checkpoint: record uncached times for each partition and coverage delta risk.

C3 `build(workspace): align module discovery with go.work and remove redundant loops`.
Objective: ensure workspace correctness and deterministic module iteration with fallback when `jq` unavailable.
Files: `scripts/list-modules.sh`, `scripts/for-each-module.sh`, `Makefile` (modules target/preflight).
Required gates: `make modules`, `make test-workspace`.
Rollback: `git revert <C3-hash>`.
MCP checkpoint: record module list diff before/after and command compatibility.

C4 `build(gates): expose deterministic commit-msg gate and pre-push aggregate`.
Objective: enforce conventional commit validation outside git hook only path.
Files: `Makefile`, `scripts/validate-commit-msg.sh`, `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (hook parity if needed).
Required gates: `make commit-msg-check MSG_FILE=.git/COMMIT_EDITMSG` (or fixture), `make quick`.
Rollback: `git revert <C4-hash>`.
MCP checkpoint: record commit-msg failure/pass examples and enforcement order.

C5 `build(docs): split docs gate into fast/local and full/strict`.
Objective: keep docs and invariants checks available and fast without dropping strict full gate.
Files: `Makefile`, `scripts/check-doc-headers.sh`, `scripts/check-doc-links.sh` (or wrapper script).
Required gates: `make docs-check-fast`, `make docs-check-full`.
Rollback: `git revert <C5-hash>`.
MCP checkpoint: record fast-vs-full duration and mismatch count.

C6 `build(replay): add dedicated replay-golden target with path-trigger rule`.
Objective: decouple replay golden from generic path and run only on sequencer/envelope/storage related changes.
Files: `Makefile`, `scripts/check-domain-isolation.sh` (if trigger helper reused), `README.md` (rule description).
Required gates: `make test-replay-golden`, `make test-workspace-race`.
Rollback: `git revert <C6-hash>`.
MCP checkpoint: record trigger matrix and 3-run flake result.

C7 `build(proto): add proto tool bootstrap and deterministic proto-check`.
Objective: prevent unnecessary proto regeneration in quick loop and enforce clean proto diffs when needed.
Files: `Makefile`, `scripts/check-registry.sh` (if reused), `scripts/proto-check.sh` (new helper).
Required gates: `make proto-tools`, `make proto-gen`, `make proto-check`, `make proto-lint`.
Rollback: `git revert <C7-hash>`.
MCP checkpoint: record proto-check dirty-tree behavior and tool version pins.

C8 `build(ci-local): tighten strong gate chain and stop-on-fail semantics`.
Objective: create deterministic local CI chain preserving strong `test-workspace` and explicit ordering.
Files: `Makefile`, `docs/rfcs/EXECUTION-SEQUENCE.md` OR `.context/plans/build-accel-w0.md` (never mixed in same commit).
Required gates: `make ci-local` (docs-check-full -> invariants-check -> test-workspace -> test-workspace-race -> proto-check conditional).
Rollback: `git revert <C8-hash>`.
MCP checkpoint: record full chain duration and first-failure stop point.

decisions.title: Validation success criteria and stop conditions
decisions.description:
Measurable success criteria:
1) `make quick` <= 4m cold and <= 2m warm on reference dev machine.
2) `make test-workspace` reduced from 31.27s uncached baseline to <= 24s uncached (>=23% improvement) without dropping packages.
3) `make test-unit` <= 90s uncached and excludes only explicit integration tests.
4) `make proto-check` deterministic: fails when proto/gen dirty, passes clean with no side-effects.
5) Replay golden stability: `make test-replay-golden` passes 5 consecutive runs with 0 flakes.

STOP CONDITIONS (require Opus Review):
S1) Any replay determinism break in golden tests.
S2) Any subject taxonomy drift (ValidateSubjectTaxonomy/subject_validation failures).
S3) Any ACK behavior outside commit boundary (ACK/NAK/TERM conformance regressions).
S4) Any gate relying on broad `skip`/masking to pass (test omission risk).
S5) Any change creating conflict between `docs/**` narrative and runtime enforcement/invariants scripts.

decisions.title: Current stop-gates recorded before commit chain execution
decisions.description: Execution chain is intentionally not started because baseline stop-gates are failing now: `make docs-check` fails (38 header compliance issues on legacy ADR/RFC docs), `make proto-lint` fails (`buf` missing), and `make proto-gen` fails (`./bin/protoc-gen-go` missing). Per rule, chain must stop on failing gate and resume only after these prerequisites are addressed in dedicated atomic commits.
